data.title: AI startup Sierra hits $100M ARR milestone: report
data.description:
• Artificial intelligence startup Sierra, led by former Salesforce (CRM) co-CEO Bret Taylor, has hit a milestone of $100M in annual recurring revenue, tech reporter Alex Heath said.
  • That's up from roughly $20M a year ago, Heath reported on his website. Like other enterprise software firms such as Salesforce, Sierra signs deals for at least 12 months — oftentimes multi-year deals — bills its customers annual and allows them to take up to 30 days before paying.
  • Sierra, which creates AI agents for customer experience teams and developers via its Agent OS, is led by Taylor. He is also the Chairman of OpenAI, a board member at Shopify (SHOP) and has worked at Meta Platforms (META) and other tech companies.
  • In September, Sierra announced it had raised $350M in new funding, which valued it at $10B.
  • Sierra did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: United Bankshares announces plan to repurchase up to 5M shares of common stock
data.description:
• United Bankshares (UBSI) announced a new plan to repurchase up to 5 million shares of common stock, or about 3.6% of the company’s total shares of common stock issued and outstanding.
  • From October 1, 2025 through November 20, 2025, United purchased 1,103,666 shares of its common stock under the 2022 Plan at a weighted average price of $35.99.
  • Shares of the company were up 4% at $37.34 on Friday.
  • Filing

data.title: Merck Keytruda formulations approved in U.S. for bladder cancer
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) on Friday approved injectable and intravenous versions of Merck’s (MRK) blockbuster anti-PD-1 therapy Keytruda for bladder cancer as part of a combination regimen with Pfizer (PFE) and Astellas’ (OTCPK:ALPMF) (OTCPK:ALPMY) Padcev.

Accordingly, intravenous Keytruda and subcutaneously delivered Keytruda Qlex will be available in the U.S. for use in combination with Padcev, an antibody-drug conjugate, for adults with muscle-invasive bladder cancer.

The treatments are indicated for neoadjuvant treatment (before surgery) followed by adjuvant treatment (after surgery) for those who are ineligible for cisplatin-based chemotherapy, according to the FDA.

The approvals were based on the companies’ KEYNOTE-905 trial, in which Keytruda plus Padcev as perioperative treatment led to statistically significant improvements in event-free survival and overall survival compared to surgery in MIBC patients for whom cisplatin is not indicated.

In October, the agency had accepted the company’s supplemental Biologics License Applications ((sBLA)) for Keytruda and Keytruda Qlex for priority review, granting April 7, 2026, as the target action date.

data.title: Azenta outlines 3%–5% organic revenue growth and 300 bps margin expansion for 2026 amid operational overhaul
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Azenta, Inc. (AZTA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO John P. Marotta highlighted the company's progress in refocusing the organization, simplifying operations, and embedding operational excellence through the Azenta Business System. He stated, "We've made considerable progress, but we have also recognized we have more to do, which excites us about the road ahead."
  • Marotta noted a major structural shift: "We have moved from a complex centralized model to one that empowers our operating companies with clear accountability and a greater agility."
  • The CEO emphasized financial strength: "The strength of our balance sheet with over $0.5 billion in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities gives us the financial flexibility to invest with discipline across 4 strategic levers."
  • Marotta announced 3% core growth and 310 basis points of margin expansion for the year, along with decisive steps to reshape the commercial organization and a focus on durable, profitable growth.
  • He shared forward-looking priorities: "We anticipate core revenue growth between 3% to 5%, and expected adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of 300 basis points, and higher free cash flow generation as we scale our operational improvements."
  • CFO Lawrence Lin stated, "Fourth quarter revenue was $159 million, up 6% year-over-year on a reported basis, and up 4% organically with Multiomics delivering a record quarter."
  • Lin reported, "Non-GAAP EPS for the fourth quarter was $0.21, and was $0.51 for the full year. I'm pleased to report an adjusted EBITDA margin of 13% in the fourth quarter, and 11.2% for the full year."
  • Lin highlighted liquidity: "We ended the year in a strong financial position with $546 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, providing us with the flexibility to invest in growth initiatives and return value to shareholders over time."

Outlook

  • The company anticipates organic revenue growth in the range of 3% to 5% for fiscal 2026.
  • Multiomics is expected to deliver low single-digit growth, while Sample Management Solutions is expected to contribute mid-single-digit growth.
  • Lin stated, "We expect a slower start in the first half of the year and anticipate first quarter revenue to decline approximately 1% to 2% year-over-year. We expect the second half of the year to accelerate as our commercial investments and growth initiatives gain traction."
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of approximately 300 basis points is targeted for fiscal 2026, with improved free cash flow generation by over 30% year-over-year.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was reported at $159 million, with 6% growth year-over-year and 4% organic growth.
  • Multiomics delivered record revenue of $73 million, representing 11% growth on a reported basis and 10% organic growth, with sequencing volume rising 50% year-over-year.
  • Sample Management Solutions revenue was $86 million, up 2% reported and flat organically, while non-GAAP gross margin for SMS rose to 49.3%.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin for the quarter was 46.7%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $21 million, with a 13% margin.
  • Free cash flow usage was $6 million for the quarter, attributed to timing of revenue and project-related billing; full-year free cash flow was $38 million.

Q&A

  • David Saxon, Needham: Asked about growth guidance, macro factors, and NIH funding impact. Marotta explained, "A lot of what we're seeing is the slowdown on capital expenditures. That's continued to impact our stores in Cryo... The government shutdown from last month is really weighing on some of the guidance."
  • Saxon: Asked about SMS growth confidence. Lin responded, "For C&I, we feel really good about kind of where we are... We expect robust growth in SRS, really through two things... Our commercial leaders close two meaningful big deals."
  • Steven Etoch, Stephens: Inquired about customer base trends and Multiomics growth components. Marotta described, "We're seeing pharma. Of course, we're tapping into the profit pools of pharma and biotech right now."
  • Andrew Cooper, Raymond James: Focused on SMS segment growth and price optimization. Marotta detailed, "You're looking at contracts of 7 to 25 years, extremely stable, reoccurring revenue is in the 90% range in that part of our portfolio."
  • Vijay Kumar, Evercore: Questioned macro impact and segment assumptions for Q1. Marotta explained the government shutdown's influence and CapEx softness. Lin added, "CapEx... that's weaker from a negative growth perspective in SMS, right?"
  • Jacqueline Kisa, TD Cowen: Asked about M&A strategy. Marotta specified a focus on tuck-ins to expand SRS, automated solutions, and synthesis.
  • Paul Knight, KeyBanc: Sought views on market growth for stores and M&A focus. Marotta said, "Stores in Cryo, we think, are low single digit right now... we're still an outgrowth story based on us capturing market share."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a slightly cautious tone, probing for specifics on macro risks, segment performance, and guidance confidence, especially regarding the impact of government funding and CapEx slowdowns.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined stance, frequently emphasizing operational improvements, investment in growth, and resilience. Marotta stated, "We are a stronger company today operationally, culturally and strategically. And we are confident in the path ahead."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' questions showed increased focus on near-term macro pressures, while management's tone became more assertive about structural changes and growth strategy despite persistent market headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 maintains a similar 3%–5% organic revenue growth range, but the company now expects a slower start in the first half of the year and a stronger acceleration in the second half.
  • Margin expansion targets increased, with 300 basis points of adjusted EBITDA margin expansion for 2026 compared to reaffirming the same in Q3.
  • Management further streamlined the organization, shifting to a decentralized model with greater regional accountability and more direct customer alignment.
  • Analysts in the current call placed heightened emphasis on macro uncertainty, government funding delays, and execution risk, while management provided greater detail on operational levers and execution progress than in the previous quarter.
  • The commercial leadership and regional go-to-market initiatives, discussed as new or developing in Q3, are now described as deeply embedded and producing measurable results.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly around capital spending and the impact of the recent government shutdown.
  • CapEx slowdown and delayed customer decisions remain key headwinds, especially affecting Cryogenic Stores and Automated Storage businesses.
  • Management is actively monitoring the macro environment and has structured new commercial and pricing initiatives to mitigate these risks.
  • Government funding delays, particularly from NIH, contributed to booking softness and are expected to push some revenue into future quarters.

Final Takeaway

Azenta's leadership underscored its transformation into a more agile, accountable, and customer-focused organization, supported by a robust balance sheet and operational discipline. While macro uncertainty and capital spending delays present headwinds, the company projects 3%–5% organic revenue growth and 300 basis points of margin expansion for fiscal 2026, with expectations for stronger performance in the second half. Strategic investments in commercial capabilities, regional leadership, and core business tuck-in acquisitions are central to driving long-term value creation and profitable growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Google rolls out ads in AI Mode results
data.description:
• Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) has started rolling out ads in AI mode for the first time ever. The company is now showing ads at the bottom of the results in AI Mode.
  • Users will be able to see relevant ads integrated into responses for queries made in AI Mode, which has now been available for a year.
  • The ads shown in AI Mode will be labelled with a 'sponsored' tag.
  • The company began testing ads in AI Mode in May this year after a positive response from showing ads in AI Overviews.
  • In a post on X, Brodie Clark, an independent SEO consultant, said that ChatGPT (MSFT), which is also planning to roll out ads, is not even close to having the same infrastructure for ads as Google does.

data.title: Banks could reap $2.6T from U.S. deregulation, Jefferies says
data.description:
Large banks could unlock some $2.6T as a result of U.S. bank deregulation that could drive lending, M&A, and tech investment, Jefferies said in a note to clients on Friday.

The capital release would come from a 15% reduction in common equity tier 1 requirements, equaling 16% of total U.S. banking assets, or half the size of JPMorgan Chase's (JPM) balance sheet, a group of Jefferies strategists and analysts led by Aniket Shah and Daniel Fannon wrote.

"The reforms are already visible, with U.S. banks increasing risk-weighted assets by 10% annualized in 25Q3 and major banks such as JPMorgan (JPM), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Wells Fargo (WFC) announcing large-scale lending, M&A, and higher profitability targets," they said. Most of the changes will come in 2026, as banks reduce management buffers and redeploy capital.

They expect the deregulation benefits to be concentrated among large global systemically important banks, or G-SIBs.  "The capital release for G-SIBs is expected to drive a 6% uplift in ROTCE for U.S. banks and a 3% uplift for UK banks," they said. "This concentration of benefits will reinforce the competitive position of the largest banks in global markets."

They note that:

  • JPMorgan (JPM) has announced a $1.5T lending target for security and resilience, including $0.5T in new loans and $10B in equity investments
  • Goldman Sachs (GS) has used excess capital for an acquisition and continues to strengthen its client-facing franchise
  • Wells Fargo (WFC) and Bank of America (BAC) have raised profitability targets and reduced management buffers. 

The U.K. is expected to follow the U.S. with a smaller and slower deregulatory push, while EU and Swiss banks "face constrained profitability and limited upside due to higher capital requirements."

Other relevant tickers: Citigroup (C), Morgan Stanley (MS), HSBC (HSBC), Barclays (BCS), Lloyds Banking Group (LYG), Deutsche Bank (DB), UBS Group (UBS), Banco Santander (SAN).

The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index (BKX) rose 1.6% in Friday midday trading. Bank of America (BAC) stock gained 1.0%, Citi (C) increased 0.8%, and Wells Fargo advanced 0.8%. JPMorgan Chase (JPM) slipped 0.4%, and Goldman Sachs (GS) was roughly flat.

data.title: Diginex signs MOU to acquire the Remedy Project
data.description:
• Diginex (NASDAQ:DGNX) announced on Friday that it has executed a non-binding memorandum of understanding to acquire The Remedy Project.
  • The proposed acquisition combines the company’s AI-powered platforms with Remedy’s proven expertise in operational-level grievance mechanisms, forced labor remediation frameworks, and access-to-remedy programs.
  • The acquisition is expected to close within the next 45 days.
  • Pursuant to the acquisition, the clients will gain automated risk scoring, escalation to verified remedy delivery, full audit trails aligned with UN and OECD guidelines, and enhanced tools that prioritize survivor safety and restitution.
  • Diginex’s memorandum of understanding to acquire Resulticks Global Companies expired on October 31, 2025.

data.title: Money managers gearing up for the Year of International Stocks
data.description:
International stocks (IDV), (VXUS) are expected to outperform U.S. equities (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI) in 2026, according to Bank of America’s Global Fund Manager Survey for November.

The survey revealed that 42% of fund managers believe international stocks will be the best performing assets in 2026, while only 22% of respondents favor U.S. stocks, highlighting a significant shift in investor sentiment.

 

 

 

The survey also indicates that the MSCI Emerging Markets index (EEM) is predicted to be the top-performing equity index by 37% of investors, followed by the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) at 13%.

“Global rebalancing theme outperformed U.S. exceptionalism as U.S. trade, industrial and geopolitical policies spurred lower oil prices and new fiscal stimulus in China, Europe, and Japan,” said Michael Hartnett, chief investment strategist at BofA.

So far in 2025, international stocks have significantly outpaced U.S. equities with gains of 24.6% compared to 11.2% for U.S. stocks.

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has been the standout performer with a remarkable 54.2% increase, while other assets showing positive returns include investment-grade bonds (PFIG) (9.3%), high-yield bonds (PHB) (9.0%), and Bitcoin (BTC-USD) (7.1%), according to the survey data.

The most overbought international equities are currently South Korean equities (EWY), up 30.7% from their 200-day moving average, followed by South Africa (EZA) (24.2%), Brazil (EWZ) (17.2%), Spain (EWP) (15.3%), and Taiwan (EWT) (15.1%). U.S. equities rank considerably lower at 13th place, just 8.3% above their 200-day moving average, BofA analysts said.

In addition, global growth expectations have turned positive for the first time this year, with a net 3% of survey respondents expecting a stronger global economy. This represents a significant improvement from the previous month when expectations stood at -8%, according to the Fund Manager survey, signaling a potential shift in the global economic outlook.

data.title: authID prices $3.67M offering; shares down nearly 22%
data.description:
• authID (AUID)  announced on Friday it has entered into a definitive agreement with investors to sell approximately 2,688,747 shares of its common stock and/or pre-funded warrants, pursuant to a registered direct offering.
  • The purchase price for one share and/or pre-funded warrant will be $1.35 ($1.71 if purchased by directors, officers and/or affiliates).
  • The aggregate gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $3.67 million before deducting placement agent fees and other offering expenses.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about November 24, 2025.
  • authID intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • AUID is -21.92% to $1.335.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Republic Technologies signs agreement for $100M secured convertible note facility
data.description:
• Republic Technologies (DOCT) announced on Friday it has entered into a definitive investment agreement with an institutional investor for a secured convertible note facility of up to $100 million.
  • The convertible facility has a term of 24 months, bears 0% interest, and includes a 10% original issue discount. It will be secured by a fixed number of ETH as collateral (the ETH Target), equal to $12 million. The ETH Target will remain fixed for the duration of the convertible facility, said the company. 
  • The Convertible Facility consists of a first drawdown, subsequent drawdowns, and potential Top-Up Tranches.
  • The First Drawdown comprises a $10 million convertible note. In connection with the First Drawdown, the company will issue to the investor common share purchase warrants, exercisable to purchase common shares at a price of C$0.50 per common share for a period of 5 years from the date of issuance. If fully exercised, these warrants would provide the company with approximately an additional $10 million in financing.
  • The First Drawdown is expected to close on or around December 5, 2025.
  • The remaining $90 million in available capacity may be accessed through subsequent drawdowns and Top-Up Tranches.
  • The company intends to allocate approximately 90% of the net proceeds of the Financing toward the acquisition of ETH to support Republic’s validator infrastructure and attestation services, with the balance to be used for general working capital purposes.
  • Source: Press Release
